text.p: SECTION A paper 2

1. The people of Owu led by prince Dejumo destroyed Ile-Ife their sacred land. their and destroyed killed the people and captured Iyunloye the wife of Okunade due to her beauty and held her captive. So Okunade vowed to revenge. he joined the Army and stayed there for years until his rank raises to that of a mayor and he held the people of Owu captives for 7 years until they came inside the city and destroyed the city and the men of Owu and the women among the soldiers. They also disobeyed the god's and the god's them.
SECTION B
There Humour simply means Laughter.There are so many humour Shaw uses in the play Arms and the man and some of them are thus:
A man loading his ammunition with chocolate instead of bullets provokes laughter
How the man (Bluntchili) slept off after the bulgarian soldiers came and searched bed chamber.
The disappearance of the coat and its reappearance.The disapperance of the portrait and inside the pocket of the coat it's reappearance 2 words provokes laughter.
SECTION C

The poem was talking about a boy who 2 words faced extreme poverty, loneliness. Stanza 2 line three (3) described how his blue shirt was due to poverty. As the , the four cardinal point meets in his head. he asked his mother where he comes from because he want to take a step forward. When he will wear long trousers because they were not allowed to wear long trousers by the white because they were blacks. 1 word During the colonial rule in South-Africa there were racial descrimination between the whites and the blacks so because of this the blacks were not allowed to work in companies, wear long trousers e.t.c. so because of this, the boy exclaimes to his mother when he will wear long trousers.

The boy also asked his mother where was his father jailed. These means that the boy don't know his father. During the colonial rule in South-Africa, A black man is not allowed to enter into a taxi with the whites if a black is found critizing the white, he will be jailed and it is these that made the boys father jailed.
SECTION A paper 3
3. A Woman IN HER PRIME BY ASARE KONADU

Pokuwaa was not fertile and she because they believe that by apeacing the gods they will be fertile. Pokuwaa made so many sacrifices to the gods () due to her unfertility so as to that the gods will bless her with children.
Another superstition was there whenever 1 word there is thunder -storm, the people of Brenhoma believe that the gods is angry with them so they ask the women to cook in other to apeace the gods.
The believe that by pouring libation on the ground, They are able to calm the gods.
SECTION B
6) Satiago was a man who was experienced in Fishing and but was unlucky. The boy (Marnolin) is the boy who was taught by the old man how to fish. A boy who was picked by the old man at 5 years.

The relationship that existed between them was a cordial relationship.(father and son It was a father and son relationship, a good relationship. The old man (Santiago) and the boy (Marnolin) discussing 2 characters about sports e.g baseball. The old man waking up from sleep and calling the boy to wake up. The boy carrying the old mans fishing tools to the sea early in the morning when there is cold, walking bare-footed.
The boy giving the old man yellow -rice, buying beer for him and buying for the old man (Santiago). The boy being so unhappy when the old man comes back with no single fish.
Another relationship that existed between them was the old man teaching the boy how to fish.
